channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Monday Morning Lights: Our midseason honors, from players of year to games of year
item.description: Bay Area News Group unveils its midseason recognition. Plus, Miramonte's backup QB overcomes nerves, leads Matadors to win; Heritage aiming for school history; Christopher's star QB does more than pass.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, Nathan Canilao, Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Patriots captain Jabrill Peppers arrested on assault, strangulation, drug charges
item.description: Police say Patriots captain Jabrill Peppers was arrested over the weekend on assault, strangulation and other charges following a disturbance at a home.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jets QB Rodgers dealing with a low ankle sprain, but Saleh says he should be fine to play
item.description: New York Jets quarterback Aaron Rodgers has a sprained lower left ankle but should be able to play against Buffalo next Monday night, coach Robert Saleh said.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Stenhouse wins Talladega race after 27-car crash
item.description: A 27-car crash that involved eight of NASCAR's 12 title contenders. A chaotic cleanup that infuriated competitors. And a surprise winner.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Castellanos wins Game 2 for Phillies with 9th-inning single, top Mets 7-6
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Nick Castellanos sometimes amazes himself with intuition he can win a game on the final swing.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Charleston's Chris Mack has fiery message for 'dumb---es' after getting criticized over post about daughter
item.description: College of Charleston coach Chris Mack tore into critics who took shots at him on social media for a post about his daughter's whereabouts during a major college football upset.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fritz and Dimitrov finally advance at rain-soaked Shanghai Masters
item.description: Two days after starting out seventh-ranked Taylor Fritz and 10th-ranked Grigor Dimitrov have finally completed their second-round matches at the Shanghai Masters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Prescott throws TD pass to Tolbert with 20 seconds left to lift Cowboys past Steelers, 20-17
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Dak Prescott drifted to his left, then drifted some more. On a seemingly endless night, what was another couple of seconds going to hurt? With a flick of the wrist as T.J.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodger Stadium fans toss balls and trash on field, interrupt Padres' 10-2 win that evens NLDS
item.description: Fans threw baseballs in the direction of San Diego left fielder Jurickson Profar and then tossed trash that caused a lengthy delay before the Padres beat the Los Angeles Dodgers 10-2 on Sunday night to even their NL Division Series at a game apiece.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How AP Top 25 voters ranked the latest poll with Alabama's loss and other upsets
item.description: Longtime Associated Press Top 25 voter Kirk Bohls of the Houston Chronicle said this week's ballot was the most difficult he's had to fill out.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Special teams swing after Moody’s injury helped sink 49ers vs. Cardinals
item.description: The high of Deommodore Lenoir's 61-yard return of a blocked field goal was followed by an injury that put place kicker Jake Moody out of the game.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Kyler Murray rallies the Cardinals past the 49ers, 24-23
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Kyler Murray's day started with a 50-yard touchdown run where he pointed to the end zone in celebration almost as soon as he crossed the line of scrimmage.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PHOTOS: Padres look to rebound in game 2 of NLDS against Dodgers
item.description: Photos: The San Diego Padres are facing off against the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 2 of the NL Division Series on Sunday, with Yu Darvish starting for the Padres.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Jets' Rodgers leaves London 'banged up' with a sprained ankle and a tough loss to Vikings
item.description: LONDON (AP) — London has not been good to Aaron Rodgers. The New York Jets quarterback has lost both times he has played there and heads home “banged up” again after throwing three interceptions in a 23-17 defeat against the Minnesota Vikings at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aggies get run over by Boise State, 62-30
item.description: BOISE (ABC4 Sports) - Ashton Jeanty ran for 186 yards and three touchdowns on 13 first-half carries, while former American Fork High School quarterback Maddux Madsen passed for 256 yards and three TDs and No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Verdugo's go-ahead single and acrobatic catch lift Yankees over Royals 6-5 in ALDS opener
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Verdugo hit a tiebreaking single in the seventh inning and saved at least one run with a sliding catch along the left-field line, boosting the New York Yankees over the Kansas City Royals 6-5 on Saturday night in their AL Division Series opener.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LeBron James reveals why he joined the Lakers
item.description: The NBA season is right around the corner, but the league's oldest player still had some time on his hands on Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Greg Landry, former Detroit Lions quarterback and assistant coach, dead at 77
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Greg Landry, the former Detroit Lions quarterback and assistant coach, has died. He was 77. The Lions announced Landry's death on social media Friday. No cause of death was given.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fans set to camp out for Cal Bears big game
item.description: (KRON) – It will be a busy weekend for the City of Berkeley as the Cal Bears football team is hosting a college game day show for the first time in over 30 years. The Bears are currently (3-1) and are looking to knock off the eighth-seed and undefeated Miami Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Viral disease outbreak leads to several horses being euthanized at Los Alamitos
item.description: An outbreak of a potentially fatal viral disease at Los Alamitos Race Course has led to seven race horses being euthanized, officials have confirmed. Announced in an advisory on Thursday, the California Horse Racing Board said it was notified of an outbreak of Equine Infectious Anemia on Sept.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Leading in a new league: Oceanside golfer wins top spot in nine matches
item.description: “Medalist of the match is basically between the two teams, the individual who has the lowest score,” explained Oceanside High School golfer Kassidy Stevens.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Zach LaVine still a Chicago Bull (for now), says he's ready to do whatever teams asks of him this season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Zach LaVine insisted he is healthy and ready to do whatever the Chicago Bulls ask of him this season. Above all, he's ready to put the drama in the past.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Baseball legend Pete Rose dies at 83
item.description: Pete Rose, Major League Baseball’s all-time hits leader who endured a lifetime ban for betting on the sport, has died at age 83, his agent Jeff Lenkov confirmed to KTLA 5 News. According to TMZ, Rose died at his home in Las Vegas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Baseball legend Pete Rose dies at 83
item.description: (KRON) -- Baseball legend Pete Rose died Monday at the age of 83. Rose's complicated legacy includes being MLB's all-time hits leader but being banned from the league for gambling on games. He died Monday morning in his Las Vegas home, TMZ said. The outlet did not reveal a cause of death.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Favre tries to expand his defamation lawsuit against Mississippi auditor over welfare spending
item.description: Favre is not facing criminal charges, but he is among more than three dozen people or businesses the state is suing to try to recover misspent money through the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families program.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Giants fire Farhan Zaidi, Buster Posey named as replacement
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The San Francisco Giants have parted ways with President of Baseball Operations Farhan Zaidi and named Buster Posey as his replacement.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4
